items.behavior: File iterators n.b. there is a slight inconsistency in the return types where some return a FileInfo iterator and some a FileMetadata iterator. The latter is more lightweight, and the discrepancy depends on how the functions tend to be used. We can introduce more variations as required.

Verify that this generated code is sufficiently up-to-date.
The size of the data appended to the encrypted file on disk. This is host-local, not sent over the wire.
The time when the inode was last changed (i.e., permissions, xattrs etc changed). This is host-local, not sent over the wire.

The local_flags fields stores flags that are relevant to the local host only. It is not part of the protocol, doesn't get sent or received (we make sure to zero it), nonetheless we need it on our struct and to be able to serialize it to/from the database.
The version_hash is an implementation detail and not part of the wire format. It is used in the old database format.
the highest sequence in this batch
the highest sequence in this batch
the highest sequence in the previous batch
The owner name and group name are set when known (i.e., could be resolved on the source device), while the UID and GID are always set as they come directly from the stat() call.
Windows file objects have a single owner, which may be a user or a group. We keep the name of that account, and a flag to indicate what type it is.
CheckPath returns nil if the folder root exists and contains the marker file
Filesystem creates a filesystem for the path and options of this folder. The fset parameter may be nil, in which case no mtime handling on top of the filesystem is provided.
RequiresRestartOnly returns a copy with only the attributes that require restart on change.

Extended attribute filter. This is a list of patterns to match (glob style), each with an action (permit or deny). First match is used. If the filter is empty, all strings are permitted. If the filter is non-empty, the default action becomes deny. To counter this, you can use the "*" pattern to match all strings at the end of the filter. There are also limits on the size of accepted attributes.

If the filter is non-empty, the default action becomes deny. To counter this, you can use the "*" pattern to match all strings at the end of the filter. There are also limits on the size of accepted attributes.

If the filter is non-empty, the default action becomes deny. To counter this, you can use the "*" pattern to match all strings at the end of the filter. There are also limits on the size of accepted attributes.

If the filter is non-empty, the default action becomes deny. To counter this, you can use the "*" pattern to match all strings at the end of the filter. There are also limits on the size of accepted attributes.

streamingCFiler implements scanner.CurrentFiler using a streaming DB iterator. Files are streamed in lexicographic order (matching FS walk order after heap-sort fix). This eliminates O(n) memory usage by not preloading all files. IMPORTANT: The FS walk MUST visit files in lexicographic order (ensured by walkfs.go heap-walk). The DB iterator returns files in ORDER BY name. As files are queried, we advance the iterator. Files in DB before the current position but not queried are considered deleted. Deleted files are collected and flushed via onDeletedBatch callback when threshold is reached. This bounds memory usage to O(deletedBatchSize) instead of O(total_deleted).
Arbitrary limit that triggers a warning on kqueue systems

newStreamingCFiler creates a streaming CurrentFiler from an ordered DB iterator. onDeletedBatch is called when deleted files batch reaches threshold. If nil, all deleted files are collected and returned by Finish().
The exists function is expected to return true for all known paths (excluding "" and ".")
true when successful and should not be retried
ScheduleForceRescan marks the file such that it gets rehashed on next scan, and schedules a scan.
doInSync allows to run functions synchronously in folder.serve from exported, asynchronously called methods.
monitorWatch starts the filesystem watching and retries every minute on failure. It should not be used except in startWatch.
restartWatch should only ever be called synchronously. If you want to use this asynchronously, you should probably use scheduleWatchRestart instead.
scanOnWatchErr schedules a full scan immediately if an error occurred while watching.
scheduleWatchRestart makes sure watching is restarted from the main for loop in a folder's Serve and thus may be called asynchronously (e.g. when ignores change).
setWatchError sets the current error state of the watch and should be called regardless of whether err is nil or not.
startWatch should only ever be called synchronously. If you want to use this asynchronously, you should probably use scheduleWatchRestart instead.
stopWatch immediately aborts watching and may be called asynchronously
Update adds the fileinfo to the batch for updating, and does a few checks. It returns false if the checks result in the file not going to be updated or removed.
CurrentFile looks up a file by advancing the iterator. Since both FS and DB are in lex order, we advance until we find the file or pass it. Files we skip over are deleted (in DB but not on disk).
Error returns any error from the iterator.
Finish drains the remaining DB entries (all are deleted) and returns unflushed deleted files.
addDeleted adds a file to the deleted list, flushing if threshold reached.
advance moves to the next DB entry.
used externally, accessible regardless of serve
Whether we've called Next at least once
Callback when batch is full (nil = collect all)
deleteQueue handles deletes by delegating to a handler and queuing directories for last.
deleteQueue handles deletes by delegating to a handler and queuing directories for last.
deleteQueue handles deletes by delegating to a handler and queuing directories for last.
deleteQueue handles deletes by delegating to a handler and queuing directories for last.

receiveOnlyFolder is a folder that does not propagate local changes outward. It does this by the following general mechanism (not all of which is implemented in this file): - Local changes are scanned and versioned as usual, but get the FlagLocalReceiveOnly bit set. - When changes are sent to the cluster this bit gets converted to the Invalid bit (like all other local flags, currently) and also the Version gets set to the empty version. The reason for clearing the Version is to ensure that other devices will not consider themselves out of date due to our change. - The database layer accounts sizes per flag bit, so we can know how many files have been changed locally. We use this to trigger a "Revert" option on the folder when the amount of locally changed data is nonzero. - To revert we take the files which have changed and reset their version counter down to zero. The next pull will replace our changed version with the globally latest. As this is a user-initiated operation we do not cause conflict copies when reverting. - When pulling normally (i.e., not in the revert case) with local changes, normal conflict resolution will apply. Conflict copies will be created, but not propagated outwards (because receive only, right). Implementation wise a receiveOnlyFolder is just a sendReceiveFolder that sets an extra bit on local changes and has a Revert method.
deleteQueue handles deletes by delegating to a handler and queuing directories for last.

It does this by the following general mechanism (not all of which is implemented in this file): - Local changes are scanned and versioned as usual, but get the FlagLocalReceiveOnly bit set. - When changes are sent to the cluster this bit gets converted to the Invalid bit (like all other local flags, currently) and also the Version gets set to the empty version. The reason for clearing the Version is to ensure that other devices will not consider themselves out of date due to our change. - The database layer accounts sizes per flag bit, so we can know how many files have been changed locally. We use this to trigger a "Revert" option on the folder when the amount of locally changed data is nonzero. - To revert we take the files which have changed and reset their version counter down to zero. The next pull will replace our changed version with the globally latest. As this is a user-initiated operation we do not cause conflict copies when reverting. - When pulling normally (i.e., not in the revert case) with local changes, normal conflict resolution will apply. Conflict copies will be created, but not propagated outwards (because receive only, right). Implementation wise a receiveOnlyFolder is just a sendReceiveFolder that sets an extra bit on local changes and has a Revert method.
blockDiff returns lists of common and missing (to transform src into tgt) blocks. Both block lists must have been created with the same block size.
populateOffsets sets the Offset field on each block
Moves the given filename to the front of the job queue
checkParent verifies that the thing we are handling lives inside a directory, and not a symlink or regular file. It also resurrects missing parent dirs.

checkToBeDeleted makes sure the file on disk is compatible with what there is in the DB before the caller proceeds with actually deleting it. I.e. non-nil error status means "Do not delete!" or "is already deleted".
copierRoutine reads copierStates until the in channel closes and performs the relevant copies when possible, or passes it to the puller routine.
Returns true when the block was successfully copied.
Returns true when the block was successfully copied. The passed buffer must be large enough to accommodate the block.
Returns true when the block was successfully copied. The passed buffer must be large enough to accommodate the block.
dbUpdaterRoutine aggregates db updates and commits them in batches no larger than 1000 items, and no more delayed than 2 seconds.
deleteDir attempts to remove a directory that was deleted on a remote
deleteDirOnDisk attempts to delete a directory. It checks for files/dirs inside the directory and removes them if possible or returns an error if it fails
deleteFile attempts to delete the given file
deleteItemOnDisk deletes the file represented by old that is about to be replaced by new.
handleDir creates or updates the given directory
handleFile queues the copies and pulls as necessary for a single new or changed file.
handleSymlink creates or updates the given symlink
pull returns true if it manages to get all needed items from peers, i.e. get the device in sync with the global state.
pullScannerRoutine aggregates paths to be scanned after pulling. The scan is scheduled once when scanChan is closed (scanning can not happen during pulling).

pullerIteration runs a single puller iteration for the given folder and returns the number items that should have been synced (even those that might have failed). One puller iteration handles all files currently flagged as needed in the folder.
renameFile attempts to rename an existing file to a destination and set the right attributes on it.
scanIfItemChanged schedules the given file for scanning and returns errModified if it differs from the information in the database. Returns nil if the file has not changed.
setPlatformData makes adjustments to the metadata that should happen for all types (files, directories, symlinks). This should be one of the last things we do to a file when syncing changes to it.
shortcutFile sets file metadata, when that's the only thing that has changed.
updateFileInfoChangeTime updates the inode change time in the FileInfo, because that depends on the current, new, state of the file on disk.
A []FileError is sent as part of an event and will be JSON serialized.
A []FileError is sent as part of an event and will be JSON serialized.
A copyBlocksState is passed to copy routine if the file has blocks to be copied.
A copyBlocksState is passed to copy routine if the file has blocks to be copied.
A copyBlocksState is passed to copy routine if the file has blocks to be copied.
A pullBlockState is passed to the puller routine for each block that needs to be fetched.
A pullBlockState is passed to the puller routine for each block that needs to be fetched.
pull errors that might be just transient
A []FileError is sent as part of an event and will be JSON serialized.
A copyBlocksState is passed to copy routine if the file has blocks to be copied.
A pullBlockState is passed to the puller routine for each block that needs to be fetched.

NewModel creates and starts a new model. The model starts in read-only mode, where it sends index information to connected peers and responds to requests for file data without altering the local folder in any way.
mapDevices returns a map of device ID to nothing for the given slice of device IDs.
mapFolders returns a map of folder ID to folder configuration for the given slice of folder configurations.

newLimitedRequestResponse takes size bytes from the limiters in order, skipping nil limiters, then returns a requestResponse of the given size. When the requestResponse is closed the limiters are given back the bytes, in reverse order.
redactPathError checks if the error is actually a os.PathError, and if yes returns a redactedError with the path removed.
something relevant changed, we should try a pull
Map returns the members as a map, e.g. used in api to serialize as JSON.
has returns true if the (dev, folder) pair is in the set
hasDevice returns true if the device is set on any folder
set adds the (dev, folder) pair to the set
AddConnection adds a new peer connection to the model. An initial index will be sent to the connected peer, thereafter index updates whenever the local folder changes.
BringToFront bumps the given files priority in the job queue.
Closed is called when a connection has been closed

Completion returns the completion status, in percent with some counters, for the given device and folder. The device can be any known device ID (including the local device) or explicitly protocol.LocalDeviceID. An empty folder string means the aggregate of all folders shared with the given device.
Connection returns if we are connected to the given device.
ConnectionStats returns a map with connection statistics for each device.
CurrentIgnores returns the currently loaded set of ignore patterns, whichever it may be. No attempt is made to load or refresh ignore patterns from disk.
DeviceStatistics returns statistics about each device
DismissPendingDevices removes the record of a specific pending device.
DismissPendingFolders removes records of pending folders. Either a specific folder / device combination, or all matching a specific folder ID if the device argument is specified as EmptyDeviceID.
FolderStatistics returns statistics about each folder
Index is called when a new device is connected and we receive their full index. Implements the protocol.Model interface.
IndexUpdate is called for incremental updates to connected devices' indexes. Implements the protocol.Model interface.
LoadIgnores loads or refreshes the ignore patterns from disk, if the folder is healthy, and returns the refreshed lines and patterns.
NeedFolderFiles returns paginated list of currently needed files in progress, queued, and to be queued on next puller iteration.
OnHello is called when an device connects to us. This allows us to extract some information from the Hello message and add it to a list of known devices ahead of any checks.
PendingDevices lists unknown devices that tried to connect.

PendingFolders lists folders that we don't yet share with the offering devices. It returns the entries grouped by folder and filters for a given device unless the argument is specified as EmptyDeviceID.
RemoteNeedFolderFiles returns paginated list of currently needed files for a remote device to become synced with a folder.
Request returns the specified data segment by reading it from local disk. Implements the protocol.Model interface.
Need to hold lock on m.mut when calling this.
Only needed for testing, use addAndStartFolderLocked instead.
checkFolderRunningRLocked returns nil if the folder is up and running and a descriptive error if not. Need to hold (read) lock on m.mut when calling this.
Need to hold lock on m.mut when calling this.
generateClusterConfig returns a ClusterConfigMessage that is correct and the set of folder passwords for the given peer device
handleAutoAccepts handles adding and sharing folders for devices that have AutoAcceptFolders set to true.
handleDeintroductions handles removals of devices/shares that are removed by an introducer device
handleIntroductions handles adding devices/folders that are shared by an introducer device
numHashers returns the number of hasher routines to use for a given folder, taking into account configuration and available CPU cores.

promoteConnections checks for devices that have connections, but where the primary connection hasn't started index handlers etc. yet, and promotes the primary connection to be the index handling one. This should be called after adding new connections, and after closing a primary device connection.

requestConnectionForDevice returns a connection to the given device, to be used for sending a request. If there is only one device connection, this is the one to use. If there are multiple then we avoid the first ("primary") connection, which is dedicated to index data, and pick a random one of the others.
mirror values from Primary, for compatibility with <1.24.0
mirror values from Primary, for compatibility with <1.24.0
mirror values from Primary, for compatibility with <1.24.0
mirror values from Primary, for compatibility with <1.24.0
device -> connection IDs (invariant: if the key exists, the value is len >= 1, with the primary connection at the start of the slice)
folder -> device -> error regarding encryption consistency (may be missing)
folder -> encryption token (may be missing, and only for encryption type folders)
folderIOLimiter limits the number of concurrent I/O heavy operations, such as scans and pulls.
globalRequestLimiter limits the amount of data in concurrent incoming requests
syncMutexMap is a type safe wrapper for a sync.Map that holds mutexes
folderDeviceSet is a set of (folder, deviceID) pairs
syncMutexMap is a type safe wrapper for a sync.Map that holds mutexes
errors about why a connection is closed
BlockSize returns the block size to use for the given file size
blocksEqual returns whether two slices of blocks are exactly the same hash and index pair wise.
IsEmpty returns true if the block is a full block of zeroes.
BlocksEqual returns true when the two files have identical block lists.
WinsConflict returns true if "f" is the one to choose when it is in conflict with "other".

isEquivalent checks that the two file infos represent the same actual file content, i.e. it does purposely not check only selected (see below) struct members. Permissions (config) and blocks (scanning) can be excluded from the comparison. Any file info is not "equivalent", if it has different - type - deleted flag - invalid flag - permissions, unless they are ignored A file is not "equivalent", if it has different - modification time (difference bigger than modTimeWindow) - size - blocks, unless there are no blocks to compare (scanning) - os data A symlink is not "equivalent", if it has different - target A directory does not have anything specific to check.
HumanString returns a permission-like string representation of the flag bits
MergeWith copies platform data from other, for platforms where it's not already set on p.
SetXattrs is a convenience method to set the extended attributes of the file for the current platform.
Xattrs is a convenience method to return the extended attributes of the file for the current platform.
The size of the data appended to the encrypted file on disk. This is host-local, not sent over the wire.
The time when the inode was last changed (i.e., permissions, xattrs etc changed). This is host-local, not sent over the wire.

The local_flags fields stores flags that are relevant to the local host only. It is not part of the protocol, doesn't get sent or received (we make sure to zero it), nonetheless we need it on our struct and to be able to serialize it to/from the database. It does carry the info to decide if the file is invalid, which is part of the protocol.
was created from a truncated file info without blocks
The owner name and group name are set when known (i.e., could be resolved on the source device), while the UID and GID are always set as they come directly from the stat() call.
BlockSizes is the list of valid block sizes, from min to max
localFlagBitNames maps flag values to characters which can be used to build a permission-like bit string for easier reading.
For each block size, the hash of a block of all zeroes
IsVersionMismatch returns true if the error is a reliable indication of a version mismatch that we might want to alert the user about.
ErrTooOldVersion is returned by ExchangeHello when the other side speaks an older, incompatible version of the protocol.
ErrUnknownMagic is returned by ExchangeHello when the other side speaks something entirely unknown.
